audios.title: Coffee Order
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hey, welcome to Starbucks! What can I get started for ya?
B: Uh, yeah, can I get a latte please?
A: Sure, what size? We got tall, grande, and venti.
B: Oh, um, grande sounds good.
A: Any flavor? Vanilla, caramel…?
B: Vanilla, please.
A: Alright, that'll be six fifty. Name?
B: Emma.
A: Thanks Emma! We'll call ya when it's ready.

audios.title: New Neighbor
audios.dialog_text:
A: Oh, hi! You just moved in next door, right?
B: Yeah! I'm Kevin. Nice to meet ya.
A: I'm Lisa. Welcome to the building! Need any help?
B: Aw thanks, I think I'm good. Just gotta unpack some boxes.
A: Well if you need anything, I'm right next door.
B: Actually, do you know where I can find a grocery store around here?
A: Yeah there's one just two blocks down. Can't miss it.
B: Awesome, thanks!

audios.title: Bus Stop
audios.dialog_text:
A: Excuse me, does the number 7 bus stop here?
B: Yeah, it does. But you just missed one like five minutes ago.
A: Oh man. How often do they come?
B: Every fifteen minutes or so. Next one should be here around 10:15.
A: Okay, that's not too bad. Thanks.
B: Where ya headed?
A: Downtown, near the library.
B: Oh yeah, the 7 will take ya right there. Just four stops.

audios.title: Ordering Pizza
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hello, Tony's Pizza. How can I help you?
B: Hi, I'd like to order a large pepperoni pizza please.
A: Large pepperoni, got it. Anything else?
B: Yeah, can I get an order of garlic bread too?
A: Sure. That's eighteen ninety five. Dine in or pickup?
B: Pickup, please.
A: Alright, it'll be ready in about twenty minutes. Name?
B: Sarah.
A: Thanks Sarah! See ya soon.

audios.title: Lost Keys
audios.dialog_text:
A: Oh no, I can't find my keys anywhere!
B: Did you check your jacket pocket?
A: Yeah, empty. I already checked my bag too.
B: When was the last time you remember having them?
A: When I came home last night, I think.
B: Maybe they're in the door. You know, sometimes you leave 'em in the lock.
A: Let me check… Oh my god, they ARE! Thank you!
B: Haha, happens to everyone.

audios.title: At the Pharmacy
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hi, I need to pick up a prescription. Name's David Chen.
B: Let me look it up. Hmm, I don't see anything under Chen.
A: Oh, maybe it's under my wife's name. Try Lisa Chen.
B: Ah, there it is! One moment please.
A: How much do I owe?
B: Let's see… your insurance covers most of it. That'll be twelve dollars.
A: Great, here ya go.
B: Here's your medicine. Take one pill twice a day with food.

audios.title: Asking for Directions
audios.dialog_text:
A: Excuse me, I'm looking for the city library. Is it around here?
B: Yeah, it's not far. You see that big building with the clock tower?
A: The brown one?
B: Yeah, that's the one. Walk straight towards it, it's right behind it.
A: So past the clock tower building?
B: Exactly. About a five-minute walk.
A: Thanks so much!
B: No problem, enjoy your day!

audios.title: Weather Talk
audios.dialog_text:
A: Whew, it's hot out there today!
B: Tell me about it. I'm melting.
A: Is it always like this in July?
B: Honestly, this is actually hotter than usual.
A: Yeah? Forecast says it might rain tonight though.
B: Really? That'd be nice. We could use it.
A: For sure. My garden is dying.
B: I bet. Well, fingers crossed!

audios.title: Buying a Ticket
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hi, two tickets for the 7 o'clock show, please.
B: Sure. That's $24 total.
A: Here you go. Oh, what row are we?
B: Row H, seats 12 and 13. Pretty center.
A: Nice! Is there a student discount by the way?
B: Actually yeah, if you show your student ID, it's $10 each instead of $12.
A: Oh great, I have mine right here.
B: Perfect, that brings it down to $20. Here's your change.

audios.title: At the Cafe
audios.dialog_text:
A: What are you gonna get?
B: Hmm, I'm thinking about the blueberry muffin and a chai latte.
A: Oh, their muffins are so good. I had one yesterday.
B: Yeah? What are you having?
A: I think I'll just get a black coffee today. Trying to cut back on sugar.
B: Good for you. But you gotta live a little too, ya know?
A: Haha, you sound like my mom.
B: Well, someone's gotta say it!

audios.title: Taking a Taxi
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hey, can you take me to 42nd Street and Broadway please?
B: Sure, hop in. Buckle up.
A: Thanks. About how long will it take?
B: With this traffic… maybe twenty minutes? Could be thirty.
A: That's fine. I'm not in a huge rush.
B: First time in the city?
A: Yeah, actually. Just visiting for a few days.
B: Nice! Well, I'll take ya the scenic route if you want. Won't cost more.
A: Sure, why not!

audios.title: Library Visit
audios.dialog_text:
A: Excuse me, how do I get a library card?
B: Sure, just fill out this form. Need to see an ID too.
A: Okay, I have my driver's license.
B: Perfect. Fill this out and I'll get you set up in a minute.
A: And how many books can I check out at a time?
B: Up to fifteen. And they're due back in three weeks.
A: That's plenty. Thanks!
B: No problem. Welcome to the library!

audios.title: Talking About Pets
audios.dialog_text:
A: Is that a new dog? I haven't seen her before.
B: Yeah! This is Luna. We adopted her last month.
A: Aw, she's adorable! What breed is she?
B: Shes a golden retriever mix. Best decision we ever made.
A: How old is she?
B: About one and a half. Still has lots of puppy energy!
A: I can tell! Look at that tail go.
B: Haha yeah, she's happy all the time. It's impossible to be sad around her.

audios.title: Compliment
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hey, I really like your jacket! Is it new?
B: Thanks! Yeah, I got it last weekend on sale.
A: It looks really good on you. Where'd you get it?
B: At the mall, that store next to the food court.
A: Oh, I know the one. Was it expensive?
B: Not really. It was like forty percent off.
A: No way! I gotta go check it out.
B: You should. They have a bunch of colors.

audios.title: Lunch Break
audios.dialog_text:
A: You wanna grab lunch? I'm starving.
B: Sure, what're you in the mood for?
A: I dunno, maybe something light. A salad or a sandwich.
B: There's that new place around the corner. I heard their sandwiches are good.
A: Oh yeah? Let's check it out.
B: Cool. I just gotta finish this email real quick.
A: No rush. Take your time.
B: Alright, done. Let's go!

audios.title: Taking a Photo
audios.dialog_text:
A: Excuse me, would you mind taking a picture of us?
B: Sure, not at all! Just press this button?
A: Yeah, just tap the screen there.
B: Okay. Say cheese! Wow, that's a good one.
A: Let me see… Oh, that's perfect! Thank you so much.
B: No problem! The lighting here is really nice.
A: It really is. Thanks again!
B: You guys have a great day!

audios.title: Grocery Shopping
audios.dialog_text:
A: Okay, what do we need from the store?
B: Let me think… we're out of milk and eggs for sure.
A: Got it. Anything else?
B: Oh, and we need bread too. For sandwiches this week.
A: Alright. Milk, eggs, bread. I'll be back in twenty.
B: Wait, can you also grab some apples? The ones we have are bad.
A: Sure, any particular kind?
B: Just get whatever looks fresh. Thanks!

audios.title: Weekend Plans
audios.dialog_text:
A: So, any plans for the weekend?
B: Yeah actually, I'm thinking about going to the beach on Saturday.
A: Oh nice! Which beach?
B: Probably Santa Monica. Wanna come?
A: I'd love to, but I've got stuff to do around the house.
B: Ah come on, it's gonna be gorgeous this weekend. Like 80 degrees.
A: Don't tempt me… Ugh, fine. You convinced me.
B: That's the spirit! I'll pick you up at nine.

audios.title: Movie Debate
audios.dialog_text:
A: Did you watch the new Marvel movie yet?
B: Yeah, I saw it opening night actually.
A: What did you think? No spoilers though!
B: Honestly? I was a little disappointed. The ending felt rushed.
A: Really? I thought it was great! The visuals were incredible.
B: Oh the visuals were amazing, no doubt. But the story, you know, it just didn't hit the same.
A: I guess I see what you mean. But still, I had a good time.
B: Yeah, I mean it wasn't bad. Just had higher expectations I guess.

audios.title: Gym Talk
audios.dialog_text:
A: Hey, long time no see at the gym! Where've you been?
B: I know, I know. I kinda fell off for a couple months.
A: Life gets busy, I get it. Good to see you back though.
B: Thanks. Gotta get back in shape before summer, ya know?
A: Haha, same here. You want a spot?
B: Actually yeah, that'd be great. I'm trying to push a little heavier today.
A: Nice, how much you benching?
B: Aiming for 185. Haven't hit that in a while.

audios.title: Cooking Attempt
audios.dialog_text:
A: I tried making pasta from scratch last night.
B: Oh cool, how'd it turn out?
A: Honestly? Total disaster. The dough was way too sticky.
B: Been there. Did you follow a recipe?
A: Yeah, but I think I added too much water.
B: Pasta dough is tricky. You kinda gotta feel it, ya know?
A: Clearly I don't have that feel yet.
B: Haha, keep practicing. Maybe start with store-bought next time.

audios.title: Concert Plans
audios.dialog_text:
A: Did you get tickets for the concert?
B: Yeah! I was able to snag two before they sold out.
A: No way! I thought they were already sold out.
B: I got lucky. Someone released theirs like five minutes after they sold out.
A: That's crazy luck. How much did they cost?
B: $120 each. Kinda pricy but it's gonna be worth it.
A: Definitely. I heard their live performances are amazing.
B: I've been waiting for this tour for like three years.

audios.title: Book Club
audios.dialog_text:
A: Did you finish the book for this month's club?
B: Almost! I have like fifty pages left.
A: What do you think so far?
B: Honestly, it started slow but now I can't put it down.
A: Right? The plot twist in chapter twelve got me.
B: I haven't gotten there yet! Don't spoil it!
A: Sorry, sorry! I won't say a word.
B: I gotta finish it tonight before the meeting tomorrow.
A: You can do it. It's worth the push.

audios.title: Car Shopping
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm thinking about getting a new car. My old one's on its last legs.
B: What are you looking at?
A: I'm torn between a hybrid and a full electric.
B: If you can charge at home, electric is the way to go. Save on gas.
A: That's true. But what about long trips?
B: The range on new ones is like 300 miles. That's plenty for most people.
A: Good point. I should probably test drive a few.
B: Definitely. Don't buy anything without driving it first.

audios.title: Doctor Visit
audios.dialog_text:
A: So doctor, what's the verdict?
B: Well, it's a mild sinus infection. Nothing too serious.
A: That's a relief. I was worried it was something worse.
B: I'm gonna prescribe you some antibiotics. Take 'em for ten days.
A: Will these make me feel better right away?
B: You should start feeling better in a couple of days, but finish the whole course.
A: Got it. Anything else I should do?
B: Drink lots of water and get plenty of rest.

audios.title: Job Hunt
audios.dialog_text:
A: How's the job search going?
B: Ugh, don't even ask. It's rough out there.
A: Yeah, I've heard. How many places have you applied to?
B: Like twenty? I've had two interviews so far.
A: That's not bad! Any callbacks?
B: One place said they'd let me know by the end of the week.
A: That's promising! What company?
B: A tech startup downtown. The culture seems really cool.
A: Fingers crossed for ya!

audios.title: Birthday Gift
audios.dialog_text:
A: I have no idea what to get my mom for her birthday.
B: What does she like?
A: She likes cooking and gardening. But she already has everything.
B: What about a cooking class experience? Like a pasta-making workshop?
A: Oh, that's actually a really good idea! She'd love that.
B: Or you could get her a really nice plant for the garden.
A: The cooking class is better. She loves trying new recipes.
B: Perfect. Problem solved!

audios.title: Traffic Jam
audios.dialog_text:
A: Ugh, I'm stuck in traffic again. This is the third day this week.
B: Where are you right now?
A: On the 405, barely moving. Gonna be late for work again.
B: That highway is always a nightmare during rush hour.
A: I know. I gotta find a different route.
B: Have you tried taking the side streets? It takes longer but at least you're moving.
A: Yeah, maybe I should. Anything's better than sitting here.
B: Or you could ask your boss about working from home a couple days.

audios.title: Dental Appointment
audios.dialog_text:
A: I have a dentist appointment tomorrow and I'm dreading it.
B: Oh I hate the dentist too. What for? Just a cleaning?
A: No, I think I have a cavity. Been feeling sensitive to cold stuff.
B: Ugh, those are the worst. But better to get it checked early.
A: Yeah, I know. I've been putting it off for like two months.
B: The longer you wait, the worse it gets. Trust me.
A: You're right. I'll just go and get it over with.
B: That's the attitude. You'll be glad you did.

audios.title: Hiking Plans
audios.dialog_text:
A: Wanna go hiking this Saturday? The weather's supposed to be perfect.
B: I'd love to, but my knee's been acting up lately.
A: Oh no, what happened?
B: I think I overdid it at the gym last week. Stupidly added too much weight.
A: That sucks. Maybe try a light trail instead? Something flat?
B: I could do a flat trail. Know any good ones?
A: Yeah, there's a nice one by the river. Super easy, like two miles.
B: That sounds doable. Let's do it.

audios.title: Home Renovation
audios.dialog_text:
A: So we finally decided to renovate the bathroom.
B: It's about time! You've been talking about that forever.
A: I know, I know. We got three quotes and picked one.
B: How much did they quote you?
A: The one we went with was twelve thousand. Mid-range.
B: That's not bad for a full bathroom redo. How long will it take?
A: About three weeks they said. We'll survive.
B: At least you have a second bathroom, right?
A: Yeah, thank goodness. Otherwise I'd be staying at a hotel.

audios.title: Flight Delay
audios.dialog_text:
A: Did you hear? Our flight's been delayed.
B: What? By how long?
A: They said at least two hours. Something about weather.
B: Oh great. And I was already dreading this layover.
A: At least we're not stuck at the gate. We can grab some food.
B: True. I saw a decent-looking restaurant past security.
A: Let's go. My treat.
B: Really? Well, that makes the delay a little better.

audios.title: Phone Upgrade
audios.dialog_text:
A: I think it's time for a new phone. This one's so slow.
B: Which one are you thinking of getting?
A: The new iPhone, I guess. But it's so expensive.
B: Yeah, they're getting ridiculous. Have you looked at Android options?
A: I've thought about switching, but I'm so used to iOS.
B: The transition's not that bad honestly. Everything syncs now anyway.
A: That's what everyone says. Maybe I'll check out the Pixel.
B: My friend has one and loves it. Way better battery life.

audios.title: Apartment Search
audios.dialog_text:
A: I've been looking for apartments all week and it's exhausting.
B: The market's crazy right now. What's your budget?
A: Trying to stay under fifteen hundred for a one-bedroom.
B: That's doable but you'll have to move fast. Good ones go within a day.
A: Yeah, I learned that the hard way. Missed out on two already.
B: Did you check out that building on Main Street?
A: Yeah, saw it yesterday. The unit was nice but no laundry in the building.
B: Oh that's a dealbreaker for sure.

audios.title: Learning to Drive
audios.dialog_text:
A: I finally signed up for driving lessons!
B: Good for you! It's about time.
A: I know, I know. I should've done this years ago.
B: Don't worry, once you get the hang of it, it's easy.
A: I hope so. I'm nervous about parallel parking.
B: Everyone is at first. Just practice and you'll get it.
A: How long did it take you to get comfortable?
B: Maybe a month of driving regularly. Then it felt natural.
A: A month? Okay, I can do that.

audios.title: Salary Negotiation
audios.dialog_text:
A: So I got the job offer, but I feel like the salary's a bit low.
B: How much did they offer?
A: Eighty-five thousand. I was hoping for at least ninety-five.
B: Did you try negotiating yet?
A: Not yet. I'm kinda nervous about it. What if they take back the offer?
B: They won't. That's a myth. Most companies expect you to negotiate.
A: Really? I always thought it might offend them.
B: Not if you do it politely. Just say you're excited but based on your experience, you were hoping for something closer to market rate.
A: Okay, I'll give it a shot. Worst they can say is no.

audios.title: Relationship Conflict
audios.dialog_text:
A: Me and my girlfriend had a huge fight last night.
B: Oh no, what happened?
A: It's stupid really. She got upset because I forgot our anniversary.
B: Oof. Yeah, that's not great. But I'm guessing there's more to it?
A: I mean, she said it's not just about the date. It's that I've been distant lately.
B: Has she got a point?
A: …Yeah, honestly, I have been distracted with work.
B: Then the anniversary thing was just the last straw, ya know?
A: You're right. I need to do better. I'll talk to her tonight.

audios.title: Startup Pitch
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm pitching to investors next week. I'm freaking out.
B: You've been preparing for this for months. You got this.
A: I know the product inside out, but the financial projections are what scare me.
B: Investors know early-stage numbers are mostly guesses. They care more about the team and the market.
A: That's reassuring. But what if they ask something I don't know?
B: Just say "That's a great question, I'll look into that" — don't make up numbers.
A: Good advice. I just wanna come across as credible.
B: You will. Just be honest and passionate. That's what sells.

audios.title: Relocation Decision
audios.dialog_text:
A: I got offered a transfer to the London office. Six months.
B: Wow, that's huge! Are you gonna take it?
A: I don't know. It'd be great for my career, but I'd miss everyone.
B: Six months goes by fast. And I can come visit!
A: You'd really do that?
B: Of course! I've always wanted to go to London.
A: That means a lot. I guess my biggest worry is being lonely.
B: You'll make friends. You're good with people. And we can video call.
A: Yeah… Okay. I think I'm gonna do it.

audios.title: Family Drama
audios.dialog_text:
A: My brother and I haven't spoken in two months. It's killing me.
B: What happened?
A: He got upset about something I said at Thanksgiving. Honestly, I don't even remember what it was.
B: So nobody's reaching out?
A: I think we're both too stubborn. Waiting for the other to apologize first.
B: Sometimes you just gotta be the bigger person.
A: I know. But what if he rejects my apology?
B: At least you tried. Regret of not trying is worse.

audios.title: Health Scare
audios.dialog_text:
A: I had a panic attack last week. Thought I was having a heart attack.
B: Oh my god, that sounds terrifying. Are you okay?
A: I'm fine now. Went to the ER and everything. They said it was just anxiety.
B: Just anxiety? That's not "just" anything. That's serious.
A: Yeah, I guess I've been ignoring the signs. Not sleeping well, always stressed.
B: You gotta take care of yourself, man. Work can wait.
A: I'm starting therapy next week.
B: Good. I'm proud of you for making that call.

audios.title: Social Media Debate
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm thinking about deleting my social media. All of it.
B: Really? Even Instagram?
A: Especially Instagram. I catch myself comparing my life to everyone else's.
B: I feel that. But isn't it also how you stay in touch with people?
A: Honestly, the people who matter, I talk to directly anyway.
B: That's fair. I tried a social media detox once and it was really freeing.
A: Right? I just need the courage to actually do it.
B: Start with a week. See how you feel. You can always come back.

audios.title: Career Change
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm seriously considering quitting my job to become a graphic designer.
B: That's a big leap. Do you have a portfolio ready?
A: I've been working on one for the past six months. Taking online courses too.
B: That's smart. Have you saved enough to cover a few months without income?
A: I've got about six months of savings. I figured that's enough runway.
B: Sounds like you've thought this through pretty well.
A: I've been thinking about it for like two years. Finally feel ready.
B: Then go for it. Life's too short to wonder "what if."

audios.title: Apology
audios.dialog_text:
A: I need to apologize to you. I've been a bad friend lately.
B: What do you mean?
A: When you were going through that breakup, I wasn't really there for you. I was so caught up in my own stuff.
B: Honestly, I did notice you were distant.
A: I'm really sorry. You deserved better. I should have been there.
B: I appreciate you saying that. It means a lot.
A: I want to do better. Can we grab coffee this week? My treat.
B: Yeah, I'd like that.

audios.title: Financial Crisis
audios.dialog_text:
A: I need to be honest with you. I'm in a bit of a financial hole.
B: What happened?
A: I lost some money investing in crypto. More than I'm comfortable admitting.
B: How bad are we talking?
A: About fifteen thousand. It was money I shouldn't have risked.
B: Okay. First, don't panic. We can figure this out.
A: I feel so stupid. Everyone said it was risky.
B: Look, what's done is done. Let's make a plan to recover.

audios.title: Moving Abroad
audios.dialog_text:
A: I got accepted into the master's program in Barcelona!
B: No way! Congratulations! That's amazing!
A: Thanks! I still can't believe it. I applied on a whim.
B: So you're actually moving to Spain?
A: Looks like it! The program starts in September.
B: That's like three months away. You gotta start packing.
A: I know! I have so much to figure out. Visa, housing, all that.
B: You'll figure it out. And I'm so jealous. Barcelona is incredible.

audios.title: Parenting Struggle
audios.dialog_text:
A: My teenager wants a phone and I don't know what to do.
B: How old is she?
A: Twelve. All her friends have one, but I worry about screen time.
B: That's tough. You don't wanna be the only parent saying no.
A: Exactly! I don't wanna isolate her socially.
B: Maybe get her a basic phone with limited features first?
A: That's a good middle ground. Like a starter phone.
B: Yeah, and set clear rules about when it can be used. Dinner time, homework time, no phones.
A: I'll talk to her about it tonight. Set some ground rules.

audios.title: Trust Issues
audios.dialog_text:
A: I found out my partner's been hiding their credit card debt from me.
B: Oh man. How much are we talking?
A: Almost twenty grand. And we're supposed to buy a house next year.
B: That's a serious betrayal of trust. How do you feel?
A: I feel… lied to. Like how can I trust them now?
B: Have you talked to them about it?
A: Briefly. They said they were embarrassed and didn't know how to tell me.
B: I get being embarrassed, but that's not something you hide from your partner.
A: Exactly. I don't know how we move forward from this.

audios.title: Life Regrets
audios.dialog_text:
A: Do you ever feel like you're running out of time to do the things you wanna do?
B: All the time. Especially as I get older.
A: I turned 35 last week and it hit me hard. I haven't traveled, haven't written that book…
B: Thirty-five is still young. You've got plenty of time.
A: But the longer I wait, the harder it gets. Responsibilities pile up.
B: Then stop waiting. Take that trip. Start that book.
A: Easier said than done, you know?
B: I know. But the regret of not trying is heavier than the effort of trying.

audios.title: Office Politics
audios.dialog_text:
A: My coworker took credit for my work in the meeting today. I'm furious.
B: What?! That's insane. What did you do?
A: Nothing. I was too shocked to say anything.
B: You gotta speak up next time. Otherwise they'll keep doing it.
A: I know, I know. I just hate confrontation.
B: It's not confrontation, it's standing up for yourself. Big difference.
A: What would you say?
B: Just say "Actually, that was my idea" or "I've been working on that for weeks." Calm and firm.
A: I'll try that next time. Hopefully there won't be a next time.

audios.title: Environmental Guilt
audios.dialog_text:
A: I've been feeling guilty about how much plastic I use.
B: It's hard to avoid though. Everything comes in plastic.
A: I know, but I feel like I should be doing more. Like bring my own bags, buy in bulk…
B: Those are good starts. Every little bit helps.
A: But is it enough? Sometimes I feel like individual actions don't matter.
B: Look, systemic change is what we really need. But individual habits shift culture.
A: That's a good way to put it.
B: Plus, if everyone thought "my actions don't matter," nothing would change.

audios.title: Grief
audios.dialog_text:
A: It's been a year since my dad passed and I still don't know how to deal with it.
B: Grief doesn't have a timeline. There's no right way to feel.
A: I know. But everyone around me seems to think I should be over it by now.
B: They don't understand unless they've been through it.
A: How did you cope when you lost your mom?
B: I talked to a therapist. It helped more than I expected.
A: Maybe I should try that.
B: It helps to have someone who just listens without judging.

audios.title: Entrepreneur Doubts
audios.dialog_text:
A: It's been six months since I started my business and I'm barely breaking even.
B: Six months is nothing. Most successful businesses take years to be profitable.
A: I know, but I'm running out of savings. What if I fail?
B: Failure is part of the process. Doesn't mean you're a failure.
A: Logically I know that. But emotionally it's hard.
B: Have you thought about getting a part-time job to extend your runway?
A: I have. I just hate the idea of splitting my focus.
B: Think of it as temporary. Just to take the pressure off.
A: Yeah… you're probably right.

audios.title: Wedding Stress
audios.dialog_text:
A: Wedding planning is making me insane. I almost snapped at my mom today.
B: Oh no. What did she do?
A: She keeps adding people to the guest list. We agreed on 120, and now she wants 150.
B: Classic mom move. Is she paying for part of it?
A: Yeah, she's contributing. That's why I feel like I can't say no.
B: You can say no. It's your wedding. Boundaries are important.
A: I know, but I don't wanna hurt her feelings.
B: It's your day at the end of it. She'll get over it.

audios.title: Imposter Syndrome
audios.dialog_text:
A: I got promoted to team lead and I feel like a total fraud.
B: That's classic imposter syndrome. You earned that promotion.
A: But there are people on my team who know way more than me.
B: You weren't promoted to know everything. You were promoted to lead.
A: I guess. It's just hard to shake the feeling.
B: Fake it till you make it. Everyone does it.
A: Even you?
B: Especially me. Every time I've leveled up in my career, I felt like I didn't belong. Eventually the feeling goes away.

audios.title: Creative Block
audios.dialog_text:
A: I haven't been able to write anything good in weeks. It's so frustrating.
B: Writer's block is real. Have you tried just writing badly on purpose?
A: What do you mean?
B: Like, give yourself permission to write something terrible. Takes the pressure off.
A: Huh. That's actually not a bad idea.
B: The point is to get something on the page. You can always edit crap.
A: True. I've been waiting for "inspiration" but it's not coming.
B: Inspiration shows up when you're working, not when you're waiting.

audios.title: Online Dating
audios.dialog_text:
A: I'm so done with dating apps. It's a complete circus.
B: Tell me about it. What happened this time?
A: Matched with someone, great conversation for a week, then they just disappeared.
B: Ghosting. Classic. People treat dating apps like a game.
A: It's exhausting. I feel like I'm wasting my time.
B: Maybe try meeting people in real life? Like through hobbies or classes.
A: Easier said than done. I work from home most days.
B: Sign up for something you enjoy. Cooking class, hiking group. Way more natural.

audios.title: Work-Life Balance
audios.dialog_text:
A: My boss emailed me at 10 PM last night expecting a response.
B: That's ridiculous. Did you reply?
A: I did. And then I couldn't fall asleep because I was thinking about work.
B: You gotta set boundaries. Just because they email late doesn't mean you have to respond.
A: I know, but I feel guilty if I don't.
B: That guilt is conditioned. Companies want you to feel that way.
A: How do you handle it?
B: I turn off notifications after 7 PM. If it's urgent, they'll call.

audios.title: Cultural Differences
audios.dialog_text:
A: I had the weirdest cultural misunderstanding with my colleague today.
B: Oh? What happened?
A: I invited him for coffee and he kept insisting on paying. I didn't know if I should let him.
B: Haha, it depends on the culture. In some places, whoever invites pays.
A: I should've known that. He's from Japan. I felt so awkward.
B: Don't worry, I'm sure he understood you didn't mean anything by it.
A: Next time I'll just let him pay and say thanks.
B: Or you can treat him next time. That's how relationships build.

audios.title: Burnout
audios.dialog_text:
A: I think I'm burned out. I have zero motivation for anything.
B: That's your body telling you to slow down.
A: But I have so much to do. Deadlines, meetings, life stuff.
B: If you crash completely, you won't be able to do any of it.
A: I know you're right. But I don't know how to stop.
B: Start small. Take one day this weekend with no work. No emails.
A: One whole day? That sounds… kind of scary actually.
B: That's exactly why you need it.

audios.title: Finding Purpose
audios.dialog_text:
A: Lately I've been wondering what the point of all this is. Work, routine, repeat.
B: Sounds like an existential crisis. Pretty normal in your thirties.
A: Is it? I thought something was wrong with me.
B: No, it's healthy to question things. Means you're growing.
A: So what do I do about it?
B: Figure out what actually matters to you. Not what society says should matter.
A: That's a big question. I don't know the answer.
B: That's okay. The questioning itself is valuable.

audios.title: My Morning Routine
audios.dialog_text: Every morning I wake up at seven. The first thing I do is drink a glass of water. Then I brush my teeth and wash my face. After that, I make coffee and eat breakfast. I usually have eggs and toast. While I eat, I check my phone for messages. Then I get dressed and leave for work. It takes me about thirty minutes to get ready.

audios.title: My Favorite Food
audios.dialog_text: My favorite food is pizza. I love how the cheese melts on top. I like pepperoni and mushrooms on my pizza. There's a pizza shop near my house. The owner knows me because I go there so much. I eat pizza maybe once a week. It's not the healthiest food, but it makes me happy. My friends and I often share a large pizza on weekends.

audios.title: A Day at the Park
audios.dialog_text: Last Saturday I went to the park. The weather was warm and sunny. I saw many people there. Some were running, some were walking their dogs. Kids were playing on the swings. I sat on a bench and read my book for an hour. Then I bought ice cream from a small truck. It was a very relaxing day. I stayed until the sun started to go down.

audios.title: My Pet Cat
audios.dialog_text: I have a cat named Mimi. She is three years old and has white fur. She likes to sleep on my bed during the day. When I come home, she runs to the door to meet me. She makes a soft sound and rubs against my legs. I feed her twice a day, morning and evening. At night, she sits on my lap while I watch TV. Having a cat is very nice.

audios.title: Learning to Ride a Bike
audios.dialog_text: I learned to ride a bike when I was seven. My dad taught me in the park near our house. At first I was scared of falling. He held the back of my bike while I pedaled. After about an hour, I could ride by myself. I felt so happy and proud. Now I ride my bike everywhere. It's great exercise and it's good for the environment too.

audios.title: My Best Friend
audios.dialog_text: My best friend is named Sarah. We met in middle school. We sat next to each other in class. Soon we started eating lunch together every day. Sarah is very funny and kind. She always listens when I have a problem. We like to watch movies and go shopping on weekends. We've been friends for over ten years now. She's like a sister to me.

audios.title: The Seasons Here
audios.dialog_text: I live in a place with four seasons. Spring is warm and flowers start to bloom. Summer is hot, so I go swimming a lot. The fall is my favorite because the leaves change color. Everything looks orange and red. Winter is cold and sometimes it snows. I stay inside and drink hot chocolate. Each season has something special. I couldn't pick just one favorite.

audios.title: Learning to Drive
audios.dialog_text: Learning to drive was harder than I expected. I mean, I thought it'd be just steering and pedals, but there's so much to pay attention to. My dad took me to an empty parking lot the first time. I was all over the place, honestly. Couldn't even stay in the lines. After a few weeks of practice, I started getting the hang of it. Parallel parking was the worst — I still avoid it if I can. But you know, once you pass the test, it all clicks. Now driving is second nature.

audios.title: My First Job
audios.dialog_text: My first job was at a coffee shop when I was sixteen. I thought it'd be fun — free coffee, chatting with customers, you know. Turns out it's a lot of work. I had to wake up at five in the morning. My feet hurt after standing for hours. And some customers can be really rude. But I learned a lot. I learned how to handle pressure and how to talk to people. The best part was the tips and the friends I made there. I stayed for about a year and a half.

audios.title: Moving to a New City
audios.dialog_text: I moved to a new city about two years ago for work. Honestly, the first few months were really tough. I didn't know anyone, and I kept getting lost. Everything felt unfamiliar and I missed my old friends a lot. But I told myself to give it time. I started going to local events and joined a gym. Slowly, I began to meet people. Now I actually love this city. There's always something to do, and I've built a good group of friends here. Moving was scary, but it was worth it.

audios.title: Why I Started Running
audios.dialog_text: I started running about six months ago. Honestly, I hated it at first. The first week I could barely run for five minutes without feeling like I was gonna pass out. But I kept at it because I really wanted to get in shape. I started following a beginner program, running three times a week. Little by little, it got easier. One day I ran for twenty minutes without stopping and I couldn't believe it. Now I actually look forward to my runs. It clears my head and makes me feel good.

audios.title: A Trip to the Beach
audios.dialog_text: Last summer my friends and I took a trip to the beach. We drove about four hours to get there. The traffic was terrible, honestly. But once we arrived and saw the ocean, it was totally worth it. We spent the whole day swimming and playing volleyball. The water was really warm. In the evening, we had a barbecue on the sand and watched the sunset. Someone brought a guitar and we sang songs. It was one of those perfect days you don't forget. We're planning to do it again this year.

audios.title: Learning English
audios.dialog_text: I've been learning English for about three years now. The beginning was pretty rough, I'm not gonna lie. Grammar rules were confusing and my pronunciation was terrible. I remember being too shy to speak because I was afraid of making mistakes. But I kept watching movies and listening to podcasts every day. I also found a language partner online. We talk once a week for an hour. My English has improved a lot, but there's still so much to learn. The key is just to keep practicing every day.

audios.title: My Morning Routine Change
audios.dialog_text: I used to hit snooze like three times every morning. I'd wake up feeling rushed and stressed. So about a month ago, I decided to change my morning routine. Now I wake up at six, fifteen minutes earlier than before. I do a quick stretch, drink some water, and read for twenty minutes. No phone in bed anymore — that was a hard habit to break. Honestly, it made a huge difference. I feel calmer and more prepared for the day. It's amazing what a small change can do.

audios.title: Why I Changed Careers
audios.dialog_text: So, I spent almost seven years in accounting. Good money, stable job, all that. But man, I was miserable. Like, every Sunday I'd get this sinking feeling thinking about Monday morning. I kept telling myself, you know, it's fine, this is just what adult life is. But eventually I couldn't ignore it anymore. So I quit. Everyone thought I was crazy. I went back to school for graphic design, which was terrifying at thirty. But honestly? Best decision I ever made. I actually enjoy my work now. Sometimes you gotta take a risk to find what really fits you.

audios.title: Traveling Alone for the First Time
audios.dialog_text: I took my first solo trip when I was twenty-three. Went to Japan for three weeks. My family thought I'd lost my mind — like, you're going alone? To a country where you don't speak the language? Honestly, I was scared too. The first couple days were overwhelming. I'd get lost, order the wrong food, you know, typical stuff. But around day four, something clicked. I realized being alone meant I could do whatever I wanted. I stayed in temples, ate at random little restaurants, made friends with locals. It completely changed how I see the world. Now I try to take a solo trip every year.

audios.title: Imposter Syndrome
audios.dialog_text: You ever feel like you're gonna get found out any minute? Like someone's gonna tap you on the shoulder and say, hey, you don't actually belong here? That's imposter syndrome, and I've dealt with it my whole career. I'd get a promotion and think, well, they made a mistake. I'd finish a project and think anyone could've done it better. It's exhausting. What helped me was talking to other people about it. Turns out, like, almost everyone feels this way. Even the people who seem super confident. I'm still working on it, but now when that voice shows up, I can at least tell it to shut up.

audios.title: Dealing with Burnout
audios.dialog_text: Last year I hit a wall. Hard. I was working twelve-hour days, skipping meals, answering emails at midnight. I thought I was being productive — turns out I was just running on empty. One morning I woke up and literally couldn't get out of bed. Like, my body just gave up. That was my wake-up call. I took a month off work, which felt impossible at first. Started seeing a therapist. Learned to actually rest, not just scroll my phone. I set boundaries now — no work after seven, no email on weekends. I wish I'd done it sooner. Burnout isn't a badge of honor, it's a warning sign.

audios.title: The Gig Economy Life
audios.dialog_text: I've been freelancing for about four years now. People think it's all freedom and flexibility, and yeah, there are perks. I work from home, set my own hours, all that. But nobody talks about the downsides. The income is unpredictable — some months are great, others you're barely scraping by. No paid time off, no health insurance, no 401k. You're constantly hunting for the next project. It's like you're always on. But honestly? I can't imagine going back to a nine-to-five. Being my own boss, choosing the work I do — that's worth the uncertainty. It's a trade-off, you know?

audios.title: What Failure Taught Me
audios.dialog_text: I started a business a few years ago with a friend. We put everything into it — savings, time, late nights, the whole deal. And it failed. Like, completely failed. We ran out of money in about a year. I felt like such a loser. I avoided my friends because I didn't want to answer the 'how's the business going' question. It took me a while to see it differently. But looking back, I learned more from that failure than from any success I've had. I learned how to handle money, how to work with people, how to bounce back. Failure's not the end — it's just really expensive tuition.

audios.title: Why I Quit Social Media
audios.dialog_text: I deleted all my social media about a year ago. Instagram, Twitter, the whole lot. People thought I was being dramatic. But honestly, it was making me miserable. I'd scroll through my feed and feel like everyone else had a perfect life while I was just, you know, existing. The comparison game is toxic. And the amount of time I wasted just mindlessly swiping — it's embarrassing to think about. After I quit, something interesting happened. I started reading more. I'd call friends instead of liking their posts. I felt less anxious. I'm not saying everyone should do it, but for me, it was one of the best decisions I ever made.
